criteria_verification.evidence: cancel_cmd calls runtime.persistence.find_active_or_recent(identifier); when None it click.echos a 'no active or recent build' message to stderr and sys.exit(2). Verified by test_cancel_of_unknown_feature_exits_non_zero_with_not_found and test_cancel_of_known_feature_resolves_to_build_id_and_calls_handler. Group C BDD scenario 'Cancelling a feature with no active or recent builds is refused' also passes via test_cancel_unknown_feature_is_refused.
criteria_verification.notes: cancel_cmd calls runtime.persistence.find_active_or_recent(identifier); when None it click.echos a 'no active or recent build' message to stderr and sys.exit(2). criteria_verification.criterion_text: `forge skip` exits non-zero with REFUSED message when handler
criteria_verification.evidence: skip_cmd has two REFUSED exits: (1) sys.exit(3) with 'REFUSED — skip not allowed unless paused' when snapshot.lifecycle != PAUSED_AT_GATE; (2) sys.exit(4) with 'REFUSED — <rationale>' when handler returns SkipStatus.REFUSED_CONSTITUTIONAL. Verified by test_skip_on_non_paused_build_exits_non_zero (Group C) and test_constitutional_refusal_exits_non_zero_with_refused_message. Group C BDD scenario 'Skipping a build that is not paused is refused' passes via test_skip_on_non_paused_is_refused.
criteria_verification.notes: skip_cmd has two REFUSED exits: (1) sys.exit(3) with 'REFUSED — skip not allowed unless paused' when snapshot.lifecycle != PAUSED_AT_GATE; (2) sys.exit(4) with 'REFUSED — <rationale>' when handler returns SkipStatus.REFUSED_CONSTITUTIONAL. Verified by test_skip_on_non_paused_build_exits_non_zero (Group C) and test_constitutional_refusal_exits_non_zero_with_refused_message. Group C BDD scenario 'Skipping a build that is not paused is refused' passes via test_skip_on_non_paused_is_refused.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Both pass `responder=os.getlogin()` to the handler — Group E
criteria_verification.evidence: cancel_cmd calls handler.handle_cancel(build_id=..., reason=reason, responder=os.getlogin()); skip_cmd calls handler.handle_skip(build_id=..., stage=..., reason=reason, responder=os.getlogin()). Added optional reason/responder kwargs to CliSteeringHandler.handle_cancel/handle_skip and a _annotate_rationale helper that appends them to the structured rationale. Verified by test_cancel_passes_os_getlogin_responder_to_handler and test_skip_passes_os_getlogin_responder_to_handler which assert the captured handler arguments include responder='alice' (after monkeypatching os.getlogin).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: BDD scenarios for Group C (cancel-unknown, skip-non-paused) and
criteria_verification.evidence: tests/bdd/test_pipeline_state_machine_cancel_skip.py binds all four PSM-011 Gherkin scenarios via @scenario decorators against features/pipeline-state-machine-and-configuration/pipeline-state-machine-and-configuration.feature. All four pass: test_skip_on_non_paused_is_refused, test_cancel_unknown_feature_is_refused, test_cancel_paused_resolves_pending_review_as_rejection, test_skip_on_flagged_stage_resumes_build_and_marks_skipped. Step functions exercise the real cancel_cmd/skip_cmd via click.testing.CliRunner with an in-memory FakeCliRuntime injected via monkeypatch.

The only outstanding ruff finding (unused typing.Mapping in cli_steering.py) is pre-existing and unrelated to this task.
